tours.name: Combo: Rijksmuseum + Van Gogh Museum Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Discover Amsterdam’s two most celebrated museums on this 5.5-hour, small-group tour. Begin your journey at the Rijksmuseum, home to the Dutch Golden Age’s greatest treasures, then continue to the Van Gogh Museum to trace the life and artistry of one of the world’s most beloved painters. Led by an expert guide, this experience offers an unparalleled look into the evolution of Dutch art.

At the Rijksmuseum, admire Rembrandt’s dramatic The Night Watch and the luminous beauty of Vermeer’s The Milkmaid. Discover the extraordinary detail of 17th-century still lifes, striking portraits, and historical artifacts that capture the spirit of the Golden Age. Your guide will reveal the stories behind these masterpieces, bringing their techniques, influences, and historical significance to life.

tours.name: Amsterdam City Highlights Bike Tour – 1.5 Hours
tours.description:
Amsterdam Essentials Bike Tour (1.5 Hours)

See the highlights of Amsterdam’s city center in just 1.5 hours on this fast, fun, and easy guided bike tour. The Amsterdam Essentials Bike Tour is ideal for travelers who want a real overview of the city without committing to a longer tour.

The Amsterdam Essentials Bike Tour starts from A-Bike Rental & Tours – Leidseplein, a central and easy-to-reach location next to one of Amsterdam’s most lively squares. From here, you will ride through both vibrant neighborhoods and quieter streets, passing key sights such as Leidseplein, Museumplein, the Magere Brug (Skinny Bridge), Rembrandtplein, and the green paths of Vondelpark. Along the way, your local guide shares short, engaging stories about Amsterdam’s history, culture, and daily life.

tours.name: Rijksmuseum Guided Museum Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us for a 2.5-hour private tour of the Rijksmuseum, Holland’s answer to the Louvre and an essential part of any Amsterdam visit. Our expert guide will lead you through the museum’s remarkable collection galleries, bringing Dutch history to life through its most iconic works.

Discover Rembrandt’s masterpiece The Night Watch and his striking portraits that that shaped Dutch art for centuries. Stand before Vermeer’s The Milkmaid and five other works by this master of light and composition in the museum’s Gallery of Honour. Savour the detail of 17th-century still lifes by the Golden Age painters. Beyond the museum's incredible catalogue of paintings, explore the museum’s magnificent 19th-century library, examine intricately crafted 17th-century dollhouses, and discover a collection of historic Dutch weaponry, all illuminated by your expert guide’s fascinating commentary.

By the end of the tour, not only will you have gained a comprehen

The Cuypers Library

tours.name: Combo: Rijksmuseum + Amsterdam History - City Center Guided Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us for an in-depth exploration of Amsterdam’s rich artistic and architectural heritage on this 5.5-hour private tour. Begin your journey at the Rijksmuseum, home to the Dutch Golden Age’s most treasured works, before strolling through the city’s historic center, where centuries of history unfold along picturesque canals and hidden courtyards. With an expert guide leading the way, experience the art, history, and culture that define the Netherlands.

At the Rijksmuseum, marvel at Rembrandt’s The Night Watch and Vermeer’s The Milkmaid, alongside masterful still lifes and striking 17th-century portraits. Discover intricate dollhouses, historic weaponry, and the museum’s 19th-century library, each offering insight into the Netherlands’ artistic and cultural evolution. Your guide will illuminate the stories behind these treasures, providing historical and artistic context that brings them to life.

After a break for lu
highlights:
Weeping Tower (Schreierstoren)
Visit the Schreierstoren, or Tower of Tears, a medieval structure from which women bid farewell to loved ones departing by ship. (10 minutes)

Zeedijk & Kolksluis
Stroll along one of Amsterdam’s oldest streets, once a sea dike that held back the waters of the IJ. At the Kolksluis, admire one of the city’s most picturesque lock bridges.

VOC Headquarters (Oost-Indisch Huis)
Step into the courtyard of the Dutch East India Company’s headquarters, the birthplace of the world’s first multinational corporation. (5 minutes)

Het Kleine Huis
Marvel at one of the narrowest houses in Amsterdam, a reminder of how high land taxes once encouraged tall, narrow architecture. (5 minutes)

Zuiderkerk
Amsterdam’s first purpose-built Protestant church, designed by Hendrick de Keyser. Its elegant tower is one of the city’s defining landmarks. (5 minutes)

Rembrandthuis
Visit the former home and studio of Rembrandt, where the great master lived and worked for nearly 20 years. (5 minutes)

National Opera & Ballet (Stopera)
A striking modern riverside complex that houses both the city hall and Amsterdam’s premier opera and ballet companies. (5 minutes)

Huis aan de Drie Grachten
A rare 17th-century canal house located at the junction of three canals, with façades facing in three directions. (5 minutes)

tours.name: History of Amsterdam - City Center Guided Walking Tour – Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us on a journey through the heart of Amsterdam, where centuries of history, trade, and culture have shaped one of Europe’s most fascinating cities. On this 2.5-hour small group (no more than 12 people) tour, you’ll uncover the stories behind its medieval origins, its rise during the Dutch Golden Age, and its transformation into the modern metropolis we see today.

Walk through Dam Square, the city’s political and historical core, surrounded by grand landmarks like the Royal Palace and the New Church. Wander past Amsterdam’s oldest canals and leaning houses, remnants of a time when merchants shaped the city's skyline. Discover Nieuwmarkt Square and the striking Waag, a medieval gate that has served as a weighing house, guildhall, and even an anatomical theatre. Along the way, explore the multicultural energy of Chinatown, stroll along the Amstel River, and admire architectural gems like the historic Begijnhof and the

tours.name: Cruise Like a Local —Explore the canals
tours.description:
Join an informative, fun, and laid-back cruise where we explore Amsterdam like locals! Our captain offers a spontaneous, non-scripted tour based on the group and time of day.

Cruise aboard our historic 1928 government boat or our 360° water taxi, with an open bar (Heineken, wine, soft drinks) and good company. The 100% electric boat is intimate, comfortable, and perfect for any weather. Hop on and fall in love with Amsterdam!

Captain Dave —Every day an adventure!

tours.name: Private Lunch or Dinner Cruise
tours.description:
Experience a romantic dinner, a peaceful business lunch, or a cozy meal with friends and family aboard the elegant, historic Salonboot Delphine or Leaudine. As you cruise through the serene, smaller canals of Amsterdam, sit back and enjoy a delicious 3-course lunch or dinner, all while taking in the city’s iconic sights during your private boat tour.

Your courses are freshly picked up from a renowned restaurant on the Prinsengracht—starting with your appetizer, followed by a second delivery of the main course and dessert. Onboard, you can select your preferred wine and drinks to complement the meal.

To make your private dining experience even more special, you can choose from three stylish table settings: Classy White, Sicilian Blue, or Fico Verde.

What's included

*The enchanting canal boat Delphine or Leaudine – A beautifully restored classic with timeless charm
*Delightful 3-course dinner – Freshly prepared and served in two courses during your private cruise
*Knowledgeable skipper – Friendly and experienced, sharing stories and insights along the way
*Beautiful setting – Cruise through the peaceful, smaller canals of Amsterdam, away from the crowds
*Well-decorated boat – Elegant table settings and tasteful decor create a warm, intimate atmosphere

What's not included

🍷 Drinks available onboard

tours.name: Evening Cruise - starts from Anne Frank House
tours.description:
Enjoy and relax during the evening cruise as you sail into the Amsterdam night.
Duration

1 hour

About

During our 60-minute evening canal cruise you will explore Amsterdam, its famous monuments, culture and rich trading history.

At night, all the bridges in the city light up making it feel like you are sailing through a fairy tale. This luxury evening cruise through Amsterdam is a romantic, unique alternative to the crowded tourist canal boats.

You depart from the Anne Frank House and cruise down the Amstel River past numerous illuminated bridges, with the Skinny Bridge as the icing on the cake. The cruise is done with a small group of people, so expect an interactive tour, with plenty of room for questions and possible recommendations for your further stay in Amsterdam.

The tour is in English.

tours.name: Grand Dutch Food & History Tour - Shared Experience
tours.description:
Enjoy a high-quality food walking tour that brings Dutch cuisine, history, and culture together as you stroll through the Jordaan and along Amsterdam’s famous canals.

Explore the city in a small group of no more than 8 guests, allowing for a comfortable, unhurried experience filled with engaging conversations about anything you’d like to know. Along the way, you’ll taste classic and modern Dutch dishes at local specialty shops, cafés, and restaurants loved by Amsterdammers.

Thanks to being a genuine Amsterdam company and our many years of close relationships with our food partners and friends—often giving us exclusive access—you’ll feel welcome everywhere and enjoy a genuine behind-the-scenes experience.

With our unique mix of professional and semi-professional guides, all connected to the city for at least 15 years, you’ll hear real, personal stories and gain true insight into everyday life in Amsterdam.

tours.name: Hello Amsterdam - Introduction Walking Tour - offered in English
tours.description:
Picture this: you land in Amsterdam, stretch your legs, and think, “Okay… where do I even start?”

This tour is your answer.

It’s a lively 2-hour walk where stories, humour, and actual local life do all the heavy lifting. No dusty lectures. No “on your left, another boring building.” Just the stuff that makes Amsterdam click — the culture, the quirks, the hidden gems, the “aha, now I get it” moments.

You finish feeling oriented, inspired, and slightly smug — like you unlocked the city’s cheat codes.

Perfect for first-timers. Or honestly, anyone who wants the city to make sense without a week of Googling.

tours.name: Shifting the Frame: a Rijksmuseum guided tour
tours.description:
Discover untold stories in this private tour of Amsterdam’s Rijksmuseum (Bonus: tickets included!)

Amsterdam’s world-famous Rijksmuseum has it all: Rembrandt, Van Gogh, Vermeer, Frans Hals. It also has some incredible female, LGBTQ+, Jewish, and BIPOC badasses hiding in plain sight. This tour encompasses both.

See the famous Dutch artists in a new light while also taking in some truly astounding works created by – and showcasing – the stories of women, LGBTQ+, Jewish, and BIPOC people in the Netherlands and beyond. You’ll see the 17th-century highlights in a new light, as well as discovering the art museum’s hidden masterpieces.

What's included

Tickets to the Rijksmuseum

Passionate and knowledgeable art historian and storyteller as your guide

What's not included

(Optional) gratuity for your guide

Itinerary
A unique Rijksmuseum tour

Our (wheelchair accessible) tour explores the Rijksmuseum through a modern lens. The art museum was built as a temple to Dutch history in the 19th century, but our understanding of that history has expanded since then. tours.name: Dutch Food & History Tour of Hoorn
tours.description:
On this private guided walking and tasting experience, you explore Hoorn, a picturesque historic town on the IJsselmeer lake.

Once a major port in the Dutch Golden Age, Hoorn played an important role in global trade and exploration. As you stroll past 17th-century buildings, cobblestone streets, and the iconic waterfront, you get a real sense of its maritime past.

Hoorn gives you the chance to experience Dutch history, culture, and food in one place. You taste high-quality Dutch specialties—such as cheese, seafood, and local bites—at places loved by locals and far from anything touristy.

What's included

•A fully private guided walking tour through the historic center of Hoorn
•Four tasting experiences featuring high-quality Dutch food and drinks
•Pick up and drop off at Hoorn Central Station if travelling by train or any location in Hoorn to your liking

Itinerary

• Meet your private guide at Hoorn Central Station if arriving by train, or at any convenient spot in town.

• Start the experience with Dutch apple pie and a drink in a cozy, authentic local bar.

• Continue with a walk through the historic center, exploring quiet courtyards and hearing stories about the people and events that shaped Hoorn.

• Enjoy a tasting of classic Dutch fish specialties—herring, kibbeling, and smoked

tours.name: Van Gogh Museum Guided Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Vincent van Gogh is one of the world’s most beloved artists, known for creating some of history’s most recognizable masterpieces. His vivid colors, emotional intensity, and bold brushwork capture both the beauty and turbulence of the human experience. Though he struggled personally and received little recognition in his lifetime, his paintings now inspire millions with their raw honesty and timeless brilliance.

On this private tour, our expert guide will lead you through Van Gogh’s artistic evolution. Discover early works like The Potato Eaters, filled with dark tones and raw realism, and witness his transformation through vibrant masterpieces such as Sunflowers, Irises, and Almond Blossom. Explore his striking self-portraits, revealing glimpses of his inner turmoil, and gain unique insights through his heartfelt letters to his brother Theo. You'll even learn about his infamous severed ear, uncovering the truth behind the myth.

By the end of this 2.5-hour tour,
highlights:
Sunflowers
Stand before one of Van Gogh’s most iconic masterpieces, where vibrant yellows and thick, dynamic brushstrokes capture the energy and warmth of his time in Arles.

The Bedroom
Step into Van Gogh’s world with The Bedroom, a tranquil yet emotionally charged depiction of his living space in Arles, famous for its distorted perspective and innovative color choices.

The Potato Eaters
Considered Van Gogh’s first masterpiece, this dark, raw depiction of peasants sharing a meal contrasts sharply with his later vibrant works, highlighting his artistic evolution.

Van Gogh’s Letters
Gain intimate insights into Van Gogh’s thoughts, passions, and struggles through his deeply personal letters to his brother Theo.

tours.name: Amsterdam Luxury Boat Canal Cruise – starts from Anne Frank House
tours.description:
Jump aboard one of our legendary canal cruises and discover Amsterdam's canals from a 100% electric boat.

It depends on the season whether you end up on an open or covered boat. Both types of boats provide a unique experience on Amsterdam waters. Enjoy the views, famous monuments and characteristic canal houses the city offers. Our boarding location can be found next to the Anne Frank House (Prinsengracht 267).

Duration

1 hour

About

The canal cruise takes about 60 minutes through Amsterdam's unique, Unesco-listed Golden Age canals. We cruise into the Jordaan, a neighbourhood known for its famous Prinsengracht, towards the romantic Skinny Bridge (English: Skinny Bridge - we'll work on your Dutch pronunciation later), the Amstel River, the 7 bridges, the impressive Golden Bend and through the 9 streets back to the Anne Frank house.

During the cruise you will learn all about our favourite bars, restaurants and other hidden gems in Amsterdam!

Our skipper & enthusiastic host knows everything about Amsterdam and no questions wil

tours.name: LGBTQ+ History Tour
tours.description:
Our Amsterdam pride tour covers the spectrum of gender identity and sexual orientation

Why did the world’s first legal gay marriage happen in Amsterdam? To answer that question, our tour explores stories of individual people from the Middle Ages through today. Their experiences challenge preconceptions and broaden our understanding of gay history. We inspire LGBTQIA+ travelers and locals alike with the stories of people throughout history who stood in their identity.

Join us for a broader view of the ‘gay capital’.

Traveling Solo?

We have a minimum of 2 people per tour, so if you have trouble finding a single availability at the time you want, you have three options:

Team up with a (new) friend to start a tour at that time!
Book a Solo Traveler Ticket, and we’ll move you to a spot on an upcoming tour.
Follow us on Instagram or Facebook where we post stories 2 days in advance if we have any openings in a scheduled tour.
What's included

tours.name: Van Gogh Museum Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Vincent van Gogh is one of the world’s most beloved artists, known for creating some of history’s most recognizable masterpieces. His vivid colors, emotional intensity, and bold brushwork capture both the beauty and turbulence of the human experience. Though he struggled personally and received little recognition in his lifetime, his paintings now inspire millions with their raw honesty and timeless brilliance.

On this intimate, small-group tour—limited to just 8 guests—our expert guide will lead you through Van Gogh’s artistic evolution. Discover early works like The Potato Eaters, filled with dark tones and raw realism, and witness his transformation through vibrant masterpieces such as Sunflowers, Irises, and Almond Blossom. Explore his striking self-portraits, revealing glimpses of his inner turmoil, and gain unique insights through his heartfelt letters to his brother Theo. You’ll even learn about his infamous severe

The Bedroom
Step into Van Gogh’s world with The Bedroom, a tranquil yet emotionally charged depiction of his living space in Arles, famous for its distorted perspective and innovative color choices.

tours.name: History of Amsterdam - City Center Guided Walking Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us on a journey through the heart of Amsterdam, where centuries of history, trade, and culture have shaped one of Europe’s most fascinating cities. On this 2.5-hour private tour, you’ll uncover the stories behind its medieval origins, its rise during the Dutch Golden Age, and its transformation into the modern metropolis we see today.

Walk through Dam Square, the city’s political and historical core, surrounded by grand landmarks like the Royal Palace and the New Church. Wander past Amsterdam’s oldest canals and leaning houses, remnants of a time when merchants shaped the city's skyline. Discover Nieuwmarkt Square and the striking Waag, a medieval gate that has served as a weighing house, guildhall, and even an anatomical theatre. Along the way, explore the multicultural energy of Chinatown, stroll along the Amstel River, and admire architectural gems like the historic Begijnhof and the iconic Skinny Bridge.

By the end of the tour, you’ll have explored Am
highlights:
Weeping Tower (Schreierstoren)
Visit the Schreierstoren, or Tower of Tears, a medieval structure from which women bid farewell to loved ones departing by ship. (10 minutes)

Zeedijk & Kolksluis
Stroll along one of Amsterdam’s oldest streets, once a sea dike that held back the waters of the IJ. At the Kolksluis, admire one of the city’s most picturesque lock bridges.

VOC Headquarters (Oost-Indisch Huis)
Step into the courtyard of the Dutch East India Company’s headquarters, the birthplace of the world’s first multinational corporation. (5 minutes)

Het Kleine Huis
Marvel at one of the narrowest houses in Amsterdam, a reminder of how high land taxes once encouraged tall, narrow architecture. (5 minutes)

Zuiderkerk
Amsterdam’s first purpose-built Protestant church, designed by Hendrick de Keyser. Its elegant tower is one of the city’s defining landmarks. (5 minutes)

Rembrandthuis
Visit the former home and studio of Rembrandt, where the great master lived and worked for nearly 20 years. (5 minutes)

National Opera & Ballet (Stopera)
A striking modern riverside complex that houses both the city hall and Amsterdam’s premier opera and ballet companies. (5 minutes)

Huis aan de Drie Grachten
A rare 17th-century canal house located at the junction of three canals, with façades facing in three directions. (5 minutes)

tours.name: Windmill, Cheese Farms, & Countryside Electric Bike Tour: 2.5 hours
tours.description:
Short on time but craving authentic Dutch countryside? This compact yet electric bike adventure takes you out of Amsterdam and into the serene Waterland region in just 2.5 hours. Ride through picturesque villages, open polders and along tranquil waterways, past a real working farm and a windmill — the same landscapes that once supplied Amsterdam with its milk, beef and dairy back in the 1600s. Your guide brings it all to life with fun stories from the country's past, making this as entertaining as it is scenic. Perfect for visitors who want an active escape from the city without sacrificing a full day.

What's included

English Speaking Guided tour

Small group experience

High Quality Electric Bike

tours.name: Private Walking Tour of Amsterdam
tours.description:
This walking tour is a great way to become familiar with Amsterdam's oldest neighborhoods, dating back to the Middle Ages. It is also great to combine with the Grand Dutch Food & History Tour, as this tour mainly leads through the Jordaan area, which was built in the early 17th century.

Along the way, you will learn about interesting topics such as the origins of Amsterdam, the city’s historical importance as a trading center, and the way of life in Amsterdam since the Middle Ages.

You will also learn how freedom of religion came to be and explore – from the outside – some of the hidden churches in the city. And, of course, you will also learn about the more liberal side of Amsterdam.

What's included

-See the smallest house in Amsterdam

tours.name: Diamond City Tour
tours.description:
A fun way to get to know Amsterdam

From monarchs to starlets and from firebrands to craftsmen, you can chart Amsterdam’s history through its most precious stone. A medieval marriage inadvertently set up the country for its war of independence. A spoiled queen tried to fight a war from her gilded carriage. An organizer improved the lives of people across the Netherlands. At the center of them all? The diamond.

This walking tour through central Amsterdam explores the history of this unique city through its diamond connections. Some of the stories are scandalous and fun. Some are inspiring and thoughtful. All are engaging while showing a new side of Amsterdam.

And ends with diamonds…

tours.description:
Descubre los pueblos con más encanto de los Países Bajos
Sea cual sea tu plan de viaje a Ámsterdam tenemos una excursión que no te puedes perder por nada del mundo: molinos de viento, diques, pueblos medievales y hasta una isla bajo el nivel del mar. Los hay que creen que por visitar Ámsterdam, ya conocen bien los Países Bajos, pero la verdad es que no pueden estar más equivocados. ¡Y es que lo mejor está por llegar! Este tour es la mezcla perfecta entre cultura, paseo y gastronomía. ¿Qué nos dices? ¿Preparado para convertirte en todo un máster del siglo de oro holandés?

A penas a 20 km de la capital encontramos la verdadera esencia de Holanda en la siempre verde campiña. Los impresionantes molinos de Zaanse Schans son sólo el aperitivo de esta aventura. Visitaremos Edam, una auténtica joya del siglo XIV rodeada de extensos prados verdes. In about 60 minutes we cruise through Amsterdam's unique Unesco-listed canals. We sail through the Old Open Harbour, where all the sailing ships left for their sea voyages across the ocean. Then we sail through the Herengracht towards the Prinsengracht and its enchanting "houseboat alley". The tour will continue to the Amstel River, where we will sail under the romantic Skinny Bridge. After this we will sail back to the Open Haven.

tours.name: Private Van Gogh Museum Private Tour
tours.description:
Discover one of the most famous and influential painters in the history of art.

Follow Vincent’s story with your private guide in our Van Gogh Museum Tour. This tour will give you an introduction to one of our most beloved Dutch Masters.

Discover the Sunflowers and the genius of Vincent van Gogh.

Van Gogh is the quintessential misunderstood genius. He was considered a madman and a failure while he was alive, but attained widespread artistic and commercial success in the decades following his death. You’ll be using both your ears to listen to engaging stories about his life and artwork as you explore the museum.

More than just a tour of his masterpieces, in this tour you will learn more about Van Gogh as a person. How did he start his painting career? How was his relationship with his family? As your guide takes you through the museum, they will provide the stories behind the paintings that go much deeper than the descriptions on the wall.

Sunflowers and Potato Eaters

Find out what inspired Vincent’s artwork, from his famous Sunflowers, his intimate self-portraits, and beautiful Blossom Trees.

On this tour, you will follow Vincent’s story from his humble upbringing, his decision to take up the brush, his unrecognised genius, to eventually his most productive times and his tragic death. Why is his art so important? How did h
highlights: Ticket to the Van Gogh Museum

tours.name: Central Station: Classic Cruise with optional drinks
tours.description:
Make the smart choice for your Amsterdam trip with this budget-friendly, 90-minute canal cruise departing near the Rijksmuseum. While standard city tours rush you through the water in just an hour, our extended 1.5-hour route lets you truly relax and explore the UNESCO-listed canals without paying premium prices.

Step aboard our 100% electric, open-air boat (maximum 24 guests). Without dirty glass windows in the way, you'll get the absolute best, unobstructed photo opportunities of iconic bridges and merchant houses. Snuggle up under our cozy blankets while your local English-speaking captain shares fascinating history, hidden gems, and typical Amsterdam humor.

tours.name: Women's History Tour
tours.description:
Get inspired on this feminist Amsterdam tour!

Meet the women who made Amsterdam. Too many tours reduce female Amsterdammers to working women and Dutch feminists when women have done everything from changing society at the highest levels to making culture to defying spiritual powers. This storytelling walk through Amsterdam’s historic center explores the way women across the centuries pursued their goals in the face of societal and structural resistance. Let them energize and empower you as you see the city from a new angle.

tours.name: Jordaan Food Tour: Special 10 Year Anniversary Edition • Private
tours.description:
Jordaan is the creative hub of Amsterdam, and the locals who call it home make it one of our favourite neighbourhoods in the city. Step away from the tourist traps into this historic district and it’s generations-old family run shops and cafes.

Taste the rich tapestry of Dutch culinary treasures, from the classic indulgence of Apple Pie and artisanal cheeses to the hearty Bitterballen and a vibrant selection of ethnic dishes, celebrating Jordaan's multicultural legacy.

Stroll beside picturesque canals and through quaint alleys with our knowledgeable local guides, experiencing the essence of Jordaan one bite at a time. Discover hidden gems and stories of this district's fascinating transformation, all while savoring flavors that are as diverse and storied as the neighborhood itself.

tours.name: Jewish History Tour
tours.description:
Walk the full story of Jewish life in Amsterdam

Hear the stories of the Jewish people who shaped Amsterdam but are sometimes overlooked. The horror of the Holocaust has a gravity, but tours that only discuss World War II erase the Jewish Amsterdammers who made our city vibrant. This small-group walking tour through Amsterdam’s historic Jewish neighborhood showcases stories of true grit, dazzling intelligence, and effective activism throughout history.

Minimum height for the E-bike is 150cm or 5'

About

On this full day private e-bike tour, you’ll take a ferry across the River IJ. Riding over dikes and along canals you'll make your way through the incredible countryside to the north of Amsterdam. Cycling beneath the endless Dutch skies, made famous by the great artists, you'll pass by rows of neat little houses all painted in green. Your guide will tell you why these pretty houses are a common feature in this area.

Making your way towards Zaandam, you will discover the famous InnTel hotel and make a stop to create your own Instagrammable moment. Then it's time to continue the journey northwards.

As you approach Zaanse Schans, you will have your very own wow moment as you get your first glimpse of the chain of windmills from across the water.

Once inside the heritage village you will have time to explore the area on foot.
As well as visiting several of the windmills, there is also a cheese farm, clog workshop, sev

tours.name: Private walking tour of Haarlem
tours.description:
Discover Haarlem: the elegant, lesser-known neighbor of Amsterdam that once rivaled it in wealth and power. Wander through cobbled streets, grand squares, and along tranquil canals as our guide shares stories of merchants and painters who shaped the Dutch Golden Age. Haarlem is also the birthplace of the famous painter Frans Hals and home to the oldest museum of the Netherlands. See and hear for yourself.

This is a private tour, so you determine the pace! It normally takes about 2 hours, but our guides are mostly very flexible.

tours.name: Private Countryside South Bike Tour
tours.description:
Distance

23 km (14 miles)

About

Our tour takes you along the Amstel River beyond the edge of town and through the polder landscape with its rectilinear ditches and dikes. We’ll show you two of the few remaining authentic windmills and visit an eccentric and entertaining Dutch farmer at his cheese farm/clog factory. At the farm, they will demonstrate the traditional art of making wooden shoes and famous Gouda cheese. Afterwards, you’ll be able to have a look around in their little gift shop and taste some free samples of authentic Dutch cheese. On the way there, we’ll also pass by a cool community of houseboats and see how it has transformed from a hippie community to one that’s loved by the upper class.

tours.name: Amsterdam Light Festival Cruise on Classic Saloon Boat
tours.description:
Experience the Amsterdam Light Festival Christmas Tour

Experience Amsterdam’s transformation into a magical winter wonderland during the darkest months of the year. As the city embraces the festive season, buildings and bridges are beautifully illuminated. This is when the Amsterdam Light Festival begins - a succession of works of art, each with its own story. This year marks the 13th edition of the festival, taking place from 28 November 2024 until 19 January 2025, with the theme "Rituals".

Rituals have traditionally helped us navigate transformations - from past to future, winter to summer, youth to adulthood. Light plays a vital role in many rituals as a vibrant symbol of joy, energy, and life. We use fireworks to usher in the new year, candles to commemorate significant events, and midsummer fires to welcome the solstice. Light adds something mystical and sacred to rituals like no other element can. And rituals unify; they bring people together. That's why, in celebration of Amsterdam's 750th anniversary, we embrace the theme "Rituals" with light, with art, and with each other.

During the Amsterdam Light Festival Christmas Tour, we pass by all these fantastic sights: artworks, illuminated buildings, playful houseboats,
highlights: Adults : €38,50
Children: €25,-

tours.name: Amsterdam Flower Boat
tours.description:
Experience Amsterdam in full bloom as you glide through the iconic canals on the Original Amsterdam Flower Boat. Immerse yourself in beauty and culture with a memorable cruise that combines Amsterdam's famous waterways with the Dutch love of flowers.

You can see Amsterdam from many angles, but for a truly unique experience, nothing compares to viewing the city from a boat adorned with vibrant flowers. Join our intimate cruise with a maximum of 26 guests for a personal journey.

Depart from our central location and float along Amsterdam's most picturesque waterways. Your passionate local guide will share beautiful stories about the city's history and its special relationship with flowers, revealing hidden gems and local insights along the way.

The 60-minute journey is 100% electric, making it an eco-friendly way to experience the city. Your cruise returns to the same central starting point, perfectly positioned for you to continue exploring Amsterdam.

Have the skipper cruise you through Amsterdam's enchanting canals surrounded by a display of flowers
Create Instagram-worthy content on the city's most photogenic boat

tours.name: Private Amsterdam Urban Adventures Bike Tour
tours.description:
Distance

12 km (8 miles)

About

Private Amsterdam’s Urban Adventure

Amsterdam is famous for its biking culture and alternative art scene. During this fun 3 hour private bike tour you’ll experience these in a relaxed group setting, with a few extra surprises along the way. With your local guide you’ll go on an Urban Adventure through parts of Amsterdam the average tourist doesn’t see. Passing by beautiful canal houses, colourful street-art, unique architecture (both old and new), centuries old industrial zones and former shipyards - you’ll discover some of the hidden urban gems of Amsterdam.

This bike tour starts near Central Station, which is ideal if you are using public transport as all of the main transport methods have stops there. Once you've been fitted out with a traditional Dutch bike, we will pedal across to the Western part of the city, exploring the 17th Century harbour area of Prinseneiland and the working class neighbourhood of Jordaan. Both of these areas have changed dramatically over the last few decades as the hipsters have moved in gentrified the neighbourhood. Whilst in this area, your guide will introduce you to ‘magnet fishing’. Who knows what you might pull out of the canal!

tours.name: Combo: Rijksmuseum + Rembrandt’s City & Home Guided Combo Tour – Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us on a 5.5-hour small-group tour that explores the masterpieces of the Dutch Golden Age, from the grand halls of the Rijksmuseum to the very streets and studio where Rembrandt lived and worked. With an expert guide leading the way, trace the evolution of Dutch art and step inside the world of its most celebrated master.

Begin your day at the Rijksmuseum, home to the Netherlands’ most treasured artworks, including Rembrandt’s The Night Watch and Vermeer’s luminous The Milkmaid. Marvel at the exquisite detail of 17th-century still lifes, where artists captured the richness of Dutch society with astonishing realism. Beyond the museum’s renowned paintings, explore a curated selection of historical artifacts, intricate dollhouses, and grand merchant portraits, each offering a glimpse into the wealth, innovation, and artistry that defined the era.

After a short break, step outside and follow in Rembrandt’s footsteps through Amsterdam’s
highlights: A 2.5 hour Semi-Private Museum Tour of the Rijksmuseum, a short break for lunch, followed by a 1.5 Hour Walking Tour and a 1 hour tour inside Rembrandt's House (with Entry Tickets included)

tours.name: Amsterdam City Bike Tour
tours.description:
This tour is the perfect way to discover our beautiful city by bike, together with some fellow travelers.

We'll tell you all about the foundation and history of Amsterdam as well as how Amsterdammers live nowadays. By taking you through undiscovered parts of the city, we'l cycle through the coolest neighbourhoods in town and show you hidden courtyards, gardens and churches galore. We'll open doors and help you to understand and experience Amsterdam like a local. And while cycling through parks and over canals, our guides will answer all your questions about the city and Dutch life in general.

Besides the many hidden gems, we will also visit some landmarks like the Westerchurch and tower, the Jordaan neighbourhood, the Vondelpark and the van Gogh and Rijks Museum.

This tour takes about 3 hours. We stop for a short break halfway to sit back and watch the world go by over coffee (or a Genever) in a nice local cafe. We cycle through town at a leisurely pace and of course, provide your wheels

tours.name: Excursión Brujas
tours.description:
Descubre un entorno medieval lleno de fantasía
Di la verdad, más de una vez has pensado: “¡vaya nombre para una ciudad!”. Pues sí, también ha pasado por nuestra cabeza, pero la primera vez que pisas Brujas te das cuenta que, realmente, es una ciudad mágica. Los canales serpentean a través de la ciudad como un collar de perlas, originando su merecido apodo de “la Venecia del Norte”. Descubre sus calles de adoquines, arcos de ladrillo, iglesias de piedra y puentes pintorescos que te transportan, directamente, al medievo.

Brujas está en la región flamenca de Bélgica, que se encuentra an unas 3 horas de Ámsterdam. Este trayecto lo haremos en autobús privado y aprendiendo cosas sobre todos los puntos de interés que vamos pasando. Llegamos a Brujas, declarado Patrimonio de la Humanidad por la UNESCO y durante 1 hora, uno de nuestros guías expertos dará a conocer los aspectos más relevantes de Brujas en grupos de máximo 24 personas. Al acabar, tienes tiempo libre para probar l

tours.name: In the Footsteps of Anne Frank: A Journey Through Amsterdam during WWII
tours.description:
Tip-Based Walking Tour of Amsterdam ✽

Join us for a moving and engaging tour through the streets of Amsterdam that follows the life of Anne Frank, exploring the story of a young girl who captured the essence of hope amid despair during World War II.

Starting in the elegant, upscale Jewish Quarter, a beautiful, lesser-known area in Amsterdam's east, we will explore some of the city's most attractive neighborhoods. With compelling stories, your expert guide will connect Anne Frank's life and writings to our journey, unraveling the chain of events of the war and how they led to her family's capture.

Along the way, we will discuss Anne Frank's early years in Amsterdam, her love for writing, and the dreams she recorded in her famous diary. We will also visit important memorials and historical sites that tell the stories of many lives affected by the Holocaust.

Please note that the tour does NOT include a visit to Anne Frank's House itself.

✽ How does a tip-based tour work?
On a tip-based tour, you don't pay for a ticket, but you decide the value of the tour by tipping your guide how much you think the tour was worth to you. This motivates us to give you the best possible experience – always!

What's included

A guided walk through
highlights: The Hollandsche Schouwburg Holocaust Memorial & Dutch Theater.
The Holocaust Museum.
The Auschwitz Monument.
The Portuguese Synagogue.

tours.name: Anne Frank’s Last Walk & See the Anne Frank House in Virtual Reality
tours.description:
Anne Frank is Amsterdam’s most famous resident. Every year millions of people travel to Amsterdam to see where Anne and her family hid from the Nazis during WW2. In this tour, we will walk along the last part of the route Anne and her family took on their journey to their hiding place, and sadly their final home. Together, we will travel back in time to the Amsterdam of WWII and experience the emotion and hope of Anne Frank during the dark days of the war as her story unfolds.

As we walk around the local area we’ll see the famous Westerkerk, the outside of the house where Anne and her family were hiding for over 2 years and the beautiful Prinsengracht. Using Anne’s own words from her diary, we will learn about Anne, the ‘secret annex’, what she could see from her refuge and the influence her story has had on people in the Netherlands and the rest of the world. Rather than a sombre story of loss and sorrow, this tour is also about tolerance and hope, because, as Anne wrote: "Don’t think of all the misery, but of all the beauty that still remains."

ANNE FRANK HOUSE IN VIRTUAL REALITY

We will finish our tour at a location near Anne Frank House. Here you will enter into the Secret Annex via the magic of a virtual reality rec

tours.name: Highlights of Dutch Food & Amsterdam History
tours.description:
Enjoy a high-quality food and history walking tour that combines Dutch cuisine, local life, and Amsterdam’s past in just 2.5 hours. This relaxed walk takes you through the charming Jordaan district and along the lively Haarlemmerdijk — one of the city’s most popular streets for food, shopping, and everyday Amsterdam life.

Led by an experienced Dutch guide, you’ll explore the city at an easy, unhurried pace while learning about Amsterdam’s history, culture, and traditions. Along the way, you’ll enjoy four carefully selected tastings that together offer a well-rounded introduction to Dutch food culture.

Explore the city at your own pace with a dedicated guide, offering an even more comfortable and unhurried experience than the shared group tour, filled with engaging conversations about anything you’d like to know.

tours.name: Anne Frank’s Amsterdam: Jewish History & WWII Semi-Private tour
tours.description:
Anne Frank’s diary is one of the most powerful and enduring accounts of life under Nazi occupation, offering profound and deeply personal insights into the struggles of Jewish families during World War II. Her story has become a universal symbol of resilience and hope in the face of the horrors of the Holocaust.

On this small group walking tour - no more than 12 guests - our expert guide will take you on a journey through Amsterdam during World War II. Beginning your journey at Anne Frank’s House (exterior only), learn about her life, the persecution of the Jewish community, and the tragic deportations that sent over 100,000 Dutch Jews to concentration camps. As you explore the Jewish Quarter and Amsterdam’s historic streets and canals, uncover stories of resistance, the devastating impact of the Hunger Winter of 1944-45, and the city’s struggle under Nazi occupation.

This Anne Frank and Amsterdam during WWII tour offers a powerful a

tours.name: Private Zaanse Schans Windmill Bike Tour
tours.description:
Nothing beats cruising the Dutch countryside on a sunny day. We'll ferry north from Amsterdam into the Waterlands—rolling green pastures dotted with windmills, cows, and ridiculously photogenic farms. Our electric bikes make the 40km ride to Zaanse Schans totally effortless as we glide through traditional farmland to one of the Netherlands' most iconic heritage sites.

Explore windmills, sample cheese, watch clog-making, or devour a Dutch pancake. On the return, we'll cruise past Zaandam's wild Inntel Hotel and the edgy NDSM wharf. Skip Amsterdam's chaotic bike lanes—this tour is pure countryside bliss.

tours.name: ABSOLUTELY AMSTERDAM The #1 Best Rated Walking Tour in Amsterdam
tours.description:
Tip-Based Walking Tour of Amsterdam ✽
Are you ready for your Amsterdam adventure?

Then join us on an exciting journey along the beautiful canals of Amsterdam!

Together with your super-local guide, you will discover how freedom and tolerance shaped the city and its culture over time and understand how these values continue to influence its liberal culture today.

Be prepared to challenge all your preconceived ideas you may have of Amsterdam because after this tour you will never look and think of Amsterdam in the same way!

“From the Red Light District to the evolution of Amsterdam as the global center of world trade in the 17th century, its transition from a republic to a monarchy, the tragedy of the Nazi occupation and its enlightened and effective attitude to drug control, your guide will stitch these diverse items together with humorous personal anecdotes and insightful commentary on the underlying social and cultural mores of the Dutch people”.

tours.name: Private Anne Frank & the Jewish History of Amsterdam Tour
tours.description:
Travel back in time with us on this private tour to learn about the life of Anne Frank and Jewish history in Amsterdam. Starting in the 16th century, Amsterdam became the safe place for Jewish families fleeing from Southern and Eastern Europe.
We will also help you to get tickets for the Anne Frank House. You will then be able to explore the Anne Frank House at your own pace, unguided.

Where are we going?

Our journey starts in the historic Jewish Quarter where in the 16th Century Jews from the Iberian peninsula fled to Amsterdam to avoid persecution. Together, we will explore the old Jewish Quarter. On our route, we will see the following:

The Portuguese Synagogue
The city’s oldest park
Our favourite street in Amsterdam
The Dutch theatre – the last stop in Amsterdam for the Holocaust victims
Monuments created by the community

You’ll also discover several hidden gems along the canals, whilst hearing stories that will make history feel within arm’s reach. After a refreshing beer or coffee in our favourite restaurant, we will take a tram to the city centre.

After a short stroll, we arrive at the Westerkerk, the building Anne could see from the attic window. We will see the famous church before our final destination the Anne Frank House. We can advise you on how to get tickets for the Anne Frank House.

What's included

Dr

tours.name: School Group City Bike Tour Amsterdam
tours.description:
In this intriguing cycling tour, we share our quirkiest stories, the interesting history of Amsterdam, and some of the city’s most iconic places. We'll see the city's most famous highlights and hidden gems of Amsterdam. You don’t have to be an experienced cyclist, although we do ask that everyone can ride a bike. We will go over easy roads so the group can get used to Dutch bikes and the traffic.

CANALS, JORDAAN AND RIVERS

Enjoy an alternative sightseeing experience of Amsterdam on our exciting bike tour of the dazzling Dutch capital. Taking quieter roads, we make sure that everyone is comfortable and confident on their bike as they ride through the city. Together you will follow our guide through quaint canals and winding cobbled streets to discover an eclectic selection of sights on and off the conventional tourist trail.
We’ll take you through the most beautiful highlights of Amsterdam. Along the way, we’ll share the stories of the development of Amsterdam, We'll cycle past canals, drawbridges, the Anne Frank House, Rijksmuseum, Amstel river and much more.

Step aboard a historical Amsterdam one of a kind clog & tuplip boat. Leemstar's Stobbegat & Alde Faenen are the only boats that can take you to hidden, narrow and authentic area's of Amsterdam which most visitors never see.

Thanks to its shallow design, the praam can navigate smaller canals and pass under low bridges where larger tour boats cannot go. This allows you to explore quiet waterways and charming neighbourhoods away from the busy main canals.

During this relaxed one-hour cruise you will enjoy the Amsterdam history told by our local skipper. Step into the giant clog and tulips.

tours.name: School Group Countryside South Bike Tour
tours.description:
Amsterdam and the Netherlands are known for cheese, bikes, canals, and windmills. On this tour, the group will discover all three as they cycle to the countryside to the south of Amsterdam.

VONDELPARK

The first stop for the tour is the lovely Vondelpark (Amsterdam’s central park). This is a quiet, yet lively park that is ideal for getting comfortable with your bike. Your friendly guide will take the group along the greenery, past ponds, fountains, and beautiful houses. In the park, you can really get a feel of the relaxed atmosphere Amsterdam is known for.

AMSTERDAMSE BOS

On leaving the park, the group will follow a dedicated bike path along the river. We stop at a lock system that shows how the Dutch handle differences in water levels for inland shipping. Across the locks, we enjoy a view of a local houseboat community. The group will gain an understanding of Dutch culture through the story of the houseboats. After a few Instagrammable moments, we pick up our bikes again.

DYKES, RIVERS AND RECLAIMED LAND

As the group make their way across the greenery, they will be biking right on top of a ‘dyke’, a key element in the fight against t

tours.name: Private All Year Round Flowers & Windmills Tour
tours.description:
Explore Holland’s Delightful Flower Gardens Throughout the Year on a Private Tour

We’ve extended the tulip season, so now you can enjoy the Netherlands' most famous flower in Winter too!

Our 8-hour flower tour has 4 options, depending on the time of year you wish to visit. This way we can bring Holland's most beautiful flowers to you, even in the coldest of months.

Our tour options are:

Keukenhof & Windmills Tour
Availability: 19th March 19th until 10th May 2026

Feel the Tulip Fever and surround yourself with millions of colourful flowers on our Private Keukenhof tour.

The ‘kitchen garden’ of Keukenhof delights millions of visitors every year with its spectacular collection of tulips displayed in its gardens and pavilions. Within Keukenhof, you will find an amazing collection of tulips, daffodils, hyacinths, irises, orchids, and many other flowers and plants too, in an overwhelming rainbow of colours and varieties.

Summer Flower Gardens
Availability: Mid May until mid-August 2026

This tour will take you on a journey of discovery to find a variety of flowers that are in full bloom during this period. Roses, hydrangeas, sunflowers, lilies, orchids and many more flowers fill gardens at this time.

Keukenhof Castle & Dahlias
Availability: mid-August until mid October 2

tours.name: Private Muiderslot, Naarden & De Haar Tour
tours.description:
Muiderslot -
In the morning, we visit one of the oldest, intact castles in the Netherlands. Built in 1285, its strategic location at the end of the Vecht river made it an ideal place to raise taxes for any boats passing by. Visitors get the opportunity to see the hidden treasures of the castle. There is also more to discover in the castle’s courtyards. On top of that, the castle pays special attention to the unique relationship the castle has had with the city of Amsterdam. And like all places in this country, the ever-lasting fight with water also plays a central role in the castle’s history.

NAARDEN You will also visit the fortress town of Naarden, which still has its double-walled star fortifications in place. The city is one of the best-preserved fortified towns in Europe. Naarden was one of the most important defence works of the New Dutch Waterline and is. Naarden is an example of a Spanish star fort with fortified walls and a moat. As you stroll through Naarden you’ll find more historical buildings and monuments, such as the St. Vitus Church, a large gothic basilica from the 14th and 15th centuries.

DE HAAR De Haar Castle is the largest castle in Holland and is located just outside Utrecht.

tours.name: Private Palace Het Loo Tour
tours.description:
Paleis Het Loo was the summer residence of stadholders, kings and queens for three centuries. William III had Het Loo Palace built on the Crown Domain Het Loo as far back as 1685. Like the other European monarchs, he wished to impress his visitors and show them how rich and distinguished he was. William and his wife, the English princess Mary Stuart, were lovers of architecture and landscape design. So when they ascended the English throne in 1689, they had splendid gardens with pavilions built that were worthy of their new status and were comparable to the gardens of their French rival at Versailles.

ROYAL HISTORY IN THE PALACE

The palace remained in the hands of the Dutch royal family, and after World War II, Queen Wilhelmina decided to spend her dotage at Het Loo Palace. After her abdication in 1948, she retired to the palace and died there in 1962. The castle has been open to the public since 1984, after extensive renovations.

During the tour, you will be able to explore 2 wings of the palace, the East, where William and Mary resided, and the West, home to Wilhelmina. You can also visit the palace roof and have an amazing view of the gardens below.

tours.name: Private Haarlem City Tour
tours.description:
At the start of the day, our driver will pick you up from your hotel and bring you to our neighbouring city (30-minute drive). With its ancient buildings, cobbled streets and winding waterways, the medieval city of Haarlem is one of the most photogenic destinations in the Netherlands. Haarlem is often referred to as a scaled-down version of its more famous neighbour. But to think of it in such terms does a disservice to a city that beats with its own vibrant pulse and overflows with a rich history, epoch-spanning architecture and a thriving cultural scene all of its own.

We will see a huge windmill, the Grote Markt and several nice hidden courtyards. We can also visit the famous Corrie Ten Boom House. The Christian family Ten Boom was hiding a lot of Jews during the dark days of World War II. No trip to Haarlem is complete without a visit to the Frans Hals museum, home to an extensive collection of his group portraits and other remarkable works of art.

tours.name: Rembrandt’s City & Home Guided Tour – Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
tours.description:
Join us on a 2.5-hour small-group tour that traces the life and legacy of Rembrandt van Rijn, Amsterdam’s most celebrated painter. Follow his journey through the heart of the city, from the grandeur of the Golden Age canals to the bustling marketplaces that inspired his masterpieces. Led by an expert guide, this walking tour brings the world of 17th-century Amsterdam to life before culminating in a guided visit to Rembrandt’s former home and studio.

Stroll along the canals that shaped his artistic vision, passing historic landmarks like the Skinny Bridge and the Blue Bridge. Walk through the Jodenbuurt (Jewish Quarter), where Rembrandt lived among Amsterdam’s thriving creative and intellectual circles. Stop at Rembrandtplein, a square dedicated to the artist’s legacy, and visit the Bloemenmarkt and Mint Tower, once part of the city’s medieval defenses.

Finally, step inside Rembrandt’s home at the Museum Het Rembrandthuis, where he

No eres el único, y a día de hoy hay tantos curiosos que el acceso ha sido limitado de varias maneras. Nuestro guía va a darte TODAS Y CADA UNA de las pautas para que no te pierdas ni uno de los detalles de la ciudad más tolerante con la prostitución y las drogas.

Conoce de manera exclusiva el casco antiguo de la ciudad de Ámsterdam
Permítenos acompañarte en un paseo imprescindible por el casco histórico de Ámsterdam. Te contaremos todos los detalles sobre cómo nació esta ciudad única, recorriendo lugares tan emblemáticos como la Plaza Dam, el castillo De Waag, el Antiguo Barrio Judío o el Mercado de la Flores. tours.name: Zaanse Schans Windmills & Zaandam: Countryside E-Bike Tour 5.5 hours
tours.description:
Nothing beats cruising the Dutch countryside on a sunny day. We'll ferry north from Amsterdam into the Waterlands—rolling green pastures dotted with windmills, cows, and ridiculously photogenic farms. Our electric bikes make the 40km ride to Zaanse Schans totally effortless as we glide through traditional farmland to one of the Netherlands' most iconic heritage sites.

Explore windmills, sample cheese, watch clog-making, or devour a Dutch pancake. On the return, we'll cruise past Zaandam's wild Inntel Hotel and the edgy NDSM wharf. Skip Amsterdam's chaotic bike lanes—this tour is pure countryside bliss.

tours.name: Private Family Bike Tour (cargobike or tandem)
tours.description:
We have developed a private bike tour in Amsterdam, especially for families with children, where it does not matter how old your children are. You will see the highlights of the city while we have enough time for fun stops for the kids.

One of our guides will travel with your family and, depending on the age of your children, we work with small bicycles, a tandem for parents and children or a real Amsterdam cargo bike.

Of course, safety comes first so we go on quiet streets and have helmets for all the kids. In addition, we have several stops that are fun for children. Depending on the age, this could be a playground, a petting zoo or a graffiti tour. And what do you think of your children in our special disco bike, where you are guaranteed to attract the attention of all Amsterdammers?

Book this tour for your family and let us know your ages, height and preferences and we'll see you soon in Amsterdam.

tours.name: Private Dutch Food Tour in Amsterdam
tours.description:
If you love food & are not scared to try new flavours, this private food tour is for you!

Join us on a stroll through Amsterdam’s most vibrant neighbourhood, the Pijp, and an opportunity to explore wonderful new flavours. We will visit several delicious hotspots where you can taste what Amsterdam has on its menu.

We will walk down the most famous street market in the Netherlands, where we tell you about the area and its significance to locals.

Along with the market, we will taste a few of the best Dutch snacks. We will tell you about the history of the beautiful Sarphatipark and we have some more tastings. We will balance flavours & cuisines and explore a mix of both sweet and savoury. Think about traditional Dutch Cheese, Herring, Poffertjes and a lot more.

The Netherlands culture might not be about food but the tastes and quality of the street foods are excellent & will give you a real flavour of what it is like to 'eat Dutch'.

Be sure to join us with an empty stomach!

tours.name: Stroopwafel Workshop - Albert Cuyp Market
tours.description:
Step into a cozy venue nestled in the charming heart of Amsterdam and allow the aroma of caramel and freshly baked dough to envelop you, setting the stage for a truly authentic Dutch experience.

Begin your workshop with an introduction from your instructor, who will share the fascinating history of the stroopwafel, a beloved Dutch specialty since the 18th century.

Don your apron and gather around as the guide demonstrates the art of crafting the perfect Stroopwafel—from mixing the dough to the precise cooking technique required to achieve that golden, crispy texture.

Then, it’s your turn to get hands-on. Roll out your dough and learn how to cook it to perfection on a traditional iron. Enjoy the highlights of the process: drizzling warm, gooey syrup —the 'stroop'— between the thin waffle layers, sealing the sweet concoction.

While your creations cool, enjoy a well-deserved break with a cup of coffee or tea, mingling with fellow baking enthusiasts. Relish the first bite of your own stroopwafel, crisp yet tender, perfectly complemented by your hot beverage.

As the workshop draws to a close, pack up one o

Coffee or tea

Would you like to hear even more during your cruise through the center of Amsterdam? No problem! At Amsterdam Circle Line, you can enjoy an audio guide in 7 languages: English, Dutch, German, Spanish, Italian, French and Portuguese.

Comfort is guaranteed: all our boats are covered and equipped with heating and toilet.

tours.name: Rijksmuseum Tour - Private
tours.description:
You’re standing in front of The Night Watch. It’s crowded, loud, and you’re pretending you know what you’re looking at.

Now imagine that same moment with your own guide quietly saying,
“Here’s what no one tells you about this painting.”

That’s this private Rijksmuseum tour: a calm, focused 2-hour walk through the museum’s most important works, translated from art history into clear, human stories and plenty of “ohhh, now I get it” moments.

You get the highlights. You skip the overwhelm. You leave feeling like you actually met the Dutch Masters — not just shuffled past them.

What's included

Private 2-hour Rijksmuseum tour

Expert local guide with deep art + history knowledge

High-energy storytelling that turns paintings into people

A curated route covering the museum’s most important works

Time for all questions, theories, and “what’s going on here?” moments

Tips for what to explore after the tour if you want to

tours.name: Amsterdam Maritime History Tour with Tastings & Museum Ticket
tours.description:
Walk Amsterdam's Golden Age waterfront with a private local guide. You'll hear the real story of the VOC — the trading empire that made Amsterdam the wealthiest city in the world — through the streets, warehouses and sailor pubs that still carry that history. Two local tastings included: Dutch genever at a traditional proeflokal and a savoury local bite. Your Maritime Museum entry ticket is covered. Private groups only — just your party and your guide.

Itinerary
Meet at the Scheepvaarthuis (Prins Hendrikkade 108) — one of Amsterdam's finest Art Nouveau buildings, built as the headquarters of the city's shipping companies
Genever tasting at a traditional Amsterdam proeflokal — Dutch gin, sailor history, and why it was standard issue on every VOC ship
Walk through the old VOC warehouse district (Entrepotdok) — once the world's busiest trading hub, now converted but still carrying the bones of the empire
Maritime Museum entry — ticket covered. One hour inside with highlights from your guide: the model fleet, the VOC archive, the navigation instruments, and the full-size replica Amsterdam ship moored outside
Second local tasting — a savoury bite with a maritime or local story, chos
highlights: Meet at the Scheepvaarthuis (Prins Hendrikkade 108) — one of Amsterdam's finest Art Nouveau buildings, built as the headquarters of the city's shipping companies
Genever tasting at a traditional Amsterdam proeflokal — Dutch gin, sailor history, and why it was standard issue on every VOC ship
Walk through the old VOC warehouse district (Entrepotdok) — once the world's busiest trading hub, now converted but still carrying the bones of the empire
Second local tasting — a savoury bite with a maritime or local story, chosen fresh by your guide
Finish at the Oostelijke Handelskade waterfront — panoramic views over the IJ with the old dock cranes in frame

tours.name: Private Keukenhof and Tulips Tour
tours.description:
Our guide will pick you up from your hotel or other accommodation (within the Amsterdam area) and together you will drive towards the famous tulip gardens of Keukenhof, which is roughly a 40-minute drive from Amsterdam.

Whilst at Keukenhof Gardens you will experience a colourful display of tulips and other flowers from over 7 million bulbs planted last autumn. Within the gardens, there are also pavilions showcasing some of the most beautiful flowers. Even on a typical Dutch Spring day, you can enjoy a variety of tulips in almost every colour you can imagine.

After several hours at Keukenhof, you will travel a short distance through the flower bulb region of Bollenstreek to the Tulip Experience.

At the Tulip Experience, you will take a dynamic journey through the history, the breeding process and the magic of the tulip. The story behind the Dutch icon is told vividly from the point of view of the tulip grower, who has been cultivating and growing tulips for three generations.

The show garden has over 4 million tulips, from over 600 different varieties. These tulips are planted so that you can get up close to the flowers for the perfect selfie and Instagram moment.

At the end of the tour, your private guide will return you back to your hotel/accommodation but not without driving through some of the beautiful T

tours.name: 10 Taste Amsterdam Food Tour on UNESCO canals and Jordaan
tours.description:
10 Tastes of Amsterdam – Food Tour by the UNESCO Canals & Jordaan (3 hrs)

Discover the flavors of Amsterdam through our top-rated food walking tour — loved by over 3,000 guests and featured on TV. With 10+ tastings, 5+ authentic stops, and stories that connect food to the city’s history, this experience goes far beyond bitterballen and stroopwafels.

We keep our groups small (max 12) so you can truly connect — with your guide, the city, and each other. No two tours are ever exactly the same. Depending on the guide, the vibe of the group, and what’s fresh that day, we mix things up. From Golden Age merchants to post-colonial snacks from Suriname, Amsterdam’s food is a reflection of its layered past — and your guide will bring it all to life.

🍽️ What You’ll Taste & Explore
Aged and young Gouda paired with Dutch port or wine
highlights: Aged and young Gouda paired with Dutch port or wine
Fresh stroopwafels from a 200-year-old bakery

tours.description:
Join Amsterdam's most relaxed and smoke-friendly canal cruise for a unique experience! Drift along the scenic waterways on the famous Cloud Boat, with your knowledgeable skipper and friendly host(ess) guiding your journey.

Cruise through Amsterdam's iconic canals on our signature smoke-friendly Cloud Boat
Learn about the rich history of marijuana decriminalization and Amsterdam's famous coffeeshop culture

You can take canal cruises in many cities around the world, but for a truly authentic Amsterdam experience, nothing compares to our "Smoke & Float" journey through the historic waterways. This laid-back cruise combines canal views with an insider's look into the city's famous coffeeshop culture.

Depart from the city center and glide through some of Amsterdam's most picturesque canals. Your voyage takes you onto the Amstel River, under the 7 bridges, and reveals hidden corners of the city that only experienced local skippers know how to find.

tours.name: Amsterdam Light Festival Private Boat Cruise
tours.description:
From November 27th to January 18th, Amsterdam transforms into a glowing open-air gallery during the 14th edition of the Amsterdam Light Festival. As evening falls, the city’s canals come alive with more than 20 light artworks created by artists from around the world.

Step aboard a luxurious semi-covered boat and admire the illuminated bridges, reflections on the water, and the light installations that line the canals.

During this 75-minute cruise, relax with unlimited beer, wine, and soft drinks, while your experienced skipper guides you through the illuminated heart of the city. Join us for an evening filled with light, art, and Amsterdam’s winter charm.

tours.name: Excursión Privada Róterdam - La Haya y Scheveningen
tours.description:
A veces, hasta nosotros nos sorprendemos de que Holanda guarde tantos tesoros en tan poco espacio. ¡Y es que aquí hay ciudades para todos los gustos! Alucinarás con la arquitectura rompedora de Róterdam. La historia entre la familia real de los Orange y Scheveningen te enganchará. Los palacios y jardines de La Haya te dejarán sin palabras. Eso sí, tú marcas el ritmo.

Conoce de la mano de un guía experto estas ciudades de la provincia de Holanda del Sur. Te proponemos un itinerario, aunque el orden de los factores no altera el producto, por lo que lo más importante es que después de esta visita, no habrá secretos para ti sobre Holanda.

tours.name: Amsterdam North Bike Tour: Scenic Villages & Windmill
tours.description:
Discover the Countryside around Amsterdam with a Local Guide!

Distance

23 km (14 miles)
Cycling experience is required for our tours! Our guides will NOT allow you to continue with the tour if you do not have enough experience on the bike. This is for the safety of you and the rest of the group.

About

On this amazing guided countryside bike tour, you’ll be taken through the beautiful Dutch countryside of Waterland to the north of Amsterdam.

Our friendly and fun local guide will take you from close by Central Station, along the IJ Boulevard where you will catch a ferry that will take you across the IJ, to Noord. Continuing on your bike, our tour takes you northwards along the North Holland Canal past a windmill and into the Dutch polder landscape with its many ditches and dikes.

As the tour heads west you will cycle through several enchanting villages, including Zunderdorp, with its natural ice rink. You'll also pass through the cute village of Ransdorp, with its many listed buildings and church tower with amazing views of the area.

Dettagli dell'attività

Il paese di Zaanse Schans è una piccola frazione del comune di Zaandam. Qui la vista scorre tranquilla tra i suoi campi e le sue casette in legno verde, visitare la fabbrica degli zoccoli di legno e assaggiare il tipico formaggio olandese.

Nella visita guidata di circa un'ora e 40 minuti visiterai insieme alla guida i punti salienti del paese e imparerai tante nuove informazioni sull'antica funzione dei mulini a vento, degli zoccoli di legno (antica calzatura usata nel lavoro dei campi) e del formaggio olandese Gouda.

La visita si concluderà con il momento degli assaggi del formaggio olandese.

INSOLITAMSTERDAM è consigliata come migliore guida italiana di Amsterdam sulla guida Marco Polo.

tours.name: E-Bike Tour Amsterdam
tours.description:
Distance

~10 km (~6miles)

About

Over the course of approximately 2.5 hours, we’ll take you through the most beautiful highlights of Amsterdam on your E-Bike. We’ll pass canals, drawbridges, the Anne Frank House, Rijksmuseum, and more. You’ll learn about Amsterdam’s rich history and the foibles that make up Amsterdam's culture. We mix this with the personal stories, observations, and opinions of your local guide. All our guides are personable, knowledgeable, and fun. We always have your safety in mind, so you can enjoy your bike ride responsibly.

Please note that to join this tour, one must have a height of at least 155cm or 5' 1"

Guests bringing their own bikes should agree to the following:

tours.description:

10 - 12 km (6 - 8 miles)

About

Over the course of 2.5 to 3 hours, we’ll take you through the most beautiful and interesting aspects of this city to learn about its rich and strange history as well as the weird foibles that make up Amsterdam's culture. We mix this with the observations and opinions of your guide. All of whom are well seasoned and eager to show you everything that makes Amsterdam so very special. On our private Bike tours, it’s possible to stop along the way at a local café for a warm or cold drink or one of our local markets, to try some Dutch treats.

If you have any special request for things you like to see or do, then simply let your guide know on the day of your tour and they will do their best to incorporate it into your tour.

Enjoy an exclusive and customizable experience in Amsterdam as you journey along the city's picturesque canals on a private boat tour. Drift along the scenic waterways with your own dedicated skipper and the freedom to design your perfect cruise.

Experience the famous canals of Amsterdam on your own private, luxury vessel
Customize your journey with catering options, beverage packages, or even a silent disco

You can visit Amsterdam's attractions from land in any city break, but for a truly Amsterdam experience, nothing compares to your own private canal cruise along the world-famous waterways. Our Private Starboard Cruise offers complete exclusivity with just your group on board.

Depart from the city center and create your own route through Amsterdam's most beautiful canals. Make your way onto the Amstel River, under the 7 bridges, and discover hidden gems with at least 1.5 hours to explore at your leisure. Your experienced skipper knows all the special spots and can customize the journey to suit your preferences.

Your experience begins with a 3-hour guided walking tour through the famous Jordaan district. Together with your local guide, you’ll explore Amsterdam’s history, culture, and everyday life, while enjoying four delicious Dutch food tastings along the way. Your guide is happy to share stories and insights about the past and present of the Netherlands, Amsterdam, and its people, and to answer any questions you may have.

After the food tour, you will be personally escorted to the departure location for a luxurious 1.5-hour private canal cruise. We ensure a smooth and comfortable boarding process, where you will be warmly welcomed on board by your own private captain.

Based on our years of experience, we know that after a delightful food tour, many guests prefer to relax and enjoy the canal cruise privately with their own group. For this reason, your guide will say goodbye at the boat. Of course, your captain will share stories during the cruise and is happy to answer any questions. If you would prefer your guide to join you on the boat, please feel free to contact us and we will gladly arrange this for you.

tours.name: Amsterdam Light Festival Rondvaart in Sloep
tours.description:
Stap aan boord met KINboat voor een unieke ervaring tijdens de veertiende editie van het Amsterdam Light Festival, met als thema "Legacy." Van 27 november 2025 tot 18 januari 2026, verlichten prachtige lichtkunstwerken de grachten van Amsterdam. Dit jaar is er extra geïnvesteerd in de kunstwerken, wat zorgt voor nog indrukwekkendere installaties.

Onze 75-minuten durende rondvaart vertrekt dagelijks vanaf het Centraal Station. Aan boord geniet je van warme drankjes (optioneel te koop) en leer je van onze deskundige gidsen meer over de betekenis van elk kunstwerk. KINboat biedt comfortabele dekens en een intieme setting, zodat je optimaal kunt genieten van het uitzicht op deze bijzondere lichtkunstwerken.

tours.description:
What sets our Van Gogh Museum tour apart is the quality of the experience — whether you join a small group or a slightly larger one. Our expert guides, with backgrounds in art history and cultural studies, don’t just show you the paintings — they bring Van Gogh’s world to life with passion and insight. Along the way, you’ll hear fascinating stories, surprising details, and have the chance to engage with your guide for a deeper understanding of the artist and his time. It’s a thoughtful, enriching way to explore the museum — far beyond a standard visit.

tours.name: Private Alkmaar & Edam, the Cheese Cities
tours.description:
The tour starts at your hotel, where your guide will meet you with your vehicle and take a short drive north of Amsterdam to the historic cheese cities of Alkmaar and Edam. Depoending what day and time of year you book, it may be possible to visit one of the cheese markets in the cities.

Alkmaar

The first stop of the day is Alkmaar, often described as 'Holland in miniature'. As you explore this medieval city centre you will quickly discover it has a timeless charm with its narrow cobbled streets and beautiful bridges crossing over the canals. It

Alkmaar is also home to the oldest cheese market in the Netherlands, which has taken place on the main square for centuries. This market runs every Friday from the end of March to the end of September, and should you be visiting the city on a Friday, our guide will happily show you this traditional event. The square also has the traditional weighing house and a cheese museum which you can visit.

Edam

The afternoon will be spent in the second cheese city of the of the day. Famous for it's round cheeses, the centre of Edam has a variety of historical buildings, including a warehouse with a floating basement. Close by is Fort Edam which forms part of the Dutch Waterlines defence system. Edam also has a famous cheese market, and should you take this tour on a Wedn

tours.name: Amsterdam Countryside Bike Tour
tours.description:
On this tour, come and cycle through the quiet, beautiful countryside around Amsterdam on a gentle 4 hour bike ride. We cover around 30 kilometres (18.5 miles) on this ride, through picturesque villages and fields filled with windmills, dams, dikes, ducks, horses, cows and abundant bird life. Escape the hustle and bustle of the city centre and enjoy a leisurely ride through the green beauty of Dutch landscapes.

The route we take is as flat as the rest of our country so it’s a nice, easy ride. In the middle, we stop for coffee in an idyllic countryside village where the apple pie is as close to perfection as there is.

After 4 hours of cycling along coastal pathways and leafy green fields, you’ll head back to the city, feeling fully relaxed, rejuvenated and windswept by the sea breezes. If it’s sunny, bring sunscreen. If it’s chilly, wrap up warm.

One thing we can definitely guarantee: you’ll have cycled far enough to have burned those apple pie calories right off.

We

tours.name: Excursión Jardines Keukenhof
tours.description:
Una inmersión en el Jardín de Europa
En esta excursión en grupo, en la que se incluye transporte de ida y vuelta, la entrada al parque y un guía de habla hispana, se visitarán los jardines de Keukenhof, situados al oeste de los Países Bajos, a tan sólo 40 minutos desde Ámsterdam. Cuenta con una extensión de más de 32 hectáreas, siendo la exposición floral más grande del mundo. Una vez en Keukenhof, uno podrá deleitarse con impresionantes paisajes adornados con la flor más representativa de los holandeses, el tulipán, además de otras variedades como jacintos, orquídeas y narcisos, entre otras.

Durante el trayecto desde Ámsterdam, un guía experto llevará a cabo una contextualización histórica para comprender la estrecha relación entre el tulipán y los Países Bajos, pasando al mismo tiempo por paisajes únicos de la geografía neerlandesa. Una vez en el parque, se realizará una visita guiada de aproximadamente 45 minutos. Al finalizar las explicaciones, se dispondrá de unas 3

tours.name: Excursión Privada Róterdam - La Haya y Delft
tours.description:
Disfruta de una excursión privada por tres de las ciudades más emblemáticas de los Países Bajos. Descubre la arquitectura moderna y los impresionantes rascacielos de Róterdam, continúa hacia La Haya para conocer la sede del gobierno neerlandés y el majestuoso ámbito institucional del país, y finaliza en Delft, un encantador pueblo histórico famoso por su cerámica azul y su ambiente tradicional. Con un guía privado a tu disposición, vivirás una experiencia cómoda, flexible y completamente personalizada.
